'''Brodie Coburn''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the service of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]] during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]].<ref name="Legacy 14" /> He flew<ref name="CSWE" /> ''Skull Three'' under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of "'''Cannon'''" in [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gunn Yage]]'s [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]], a [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]]. In [[137 ABY]], Skull Squadron was stationed on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] when [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]] attempted to rescue the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Hosk Trey'lis]] from the [[Temple of the Sith]]. Given orders to capture Skywalker alive, Coburn and the Skulls engaged the former Jedi's [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], in the skies of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] until Skywalker managed to lose his pursuers in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|undercity]].<ref name="Legacy 14" />

Coburn flew against the ''Mynock'' once again when a group of Skywalker's allies, including his mother, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]]—acting in disguise so she would not be recognized—piloted the ship as they attempted to rescue Skywalker from the Sith Temple where he was being held captive. Just as the Skulls started to wear down the ''Mynock'', they were given orders to abort the mission by Captain Yage's father, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rulf Yage]].<ref name="Legacy 19">[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]]</ref> Coburn later participated, along with the rest of the squadron, in [[Operation: Thunderstroke]].<ref name="Legacy 44">[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]]</ref>
